title: HTML Headings: Structuring Content with h1 to h6

meta.title: HTML Headings Guide – h1 to h6 Explained (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to use HTML heading tags (h1 to h6) to structure content, improve readability, and boost SEO.

content.value: HTML headings are used to define titles and subtitles on a webpage. They range from <h1> (most important) to <h6> (least important).
Headings should be used in a proper hierarchy. Start with <h1>, followed by <h2>, <h3>, and so on. Avoid skipping levels for better accessibility and SEO.
Search engines use headings to understand page structure. Proper use of <h1> and subheadings improves ranking and readability.

value: **Use headings for structure, not just styling**
**Using headings for styling instead of CSS**

content.value: HTML headings play a crucial role in structuring content and improving SEO. Proper usage ensures better readability and accessibility.

faq.question: Can I use multiple h1 tags?
faq.answer: Technically yes, but it's best practice to use only one h1 per page.

faq.question: Do headings affect SEO?
faq.answer: Yes, headings help search engines understand content structure.

faq.question: Can I style headings with CSS?
faq.answer: Yes, CSS is commonly used to style headings.

quiz.question: Which is the most important heading?
How many h1 tags are recommended per page?
